source: App-authored formula drills for Basic electronics and theory.

drills.title: Ohm's law: current
drills.question: A 12 V source is connected across a 6 ohm resistor. What current flows?
drills.explanation: Use I = E / R. 12 V / 6 ohms = 2 A.

drills.title: Ohm's law: resistance
drills.question: A circuit draws 0.5 A from a 12 V supply. What is the resistance?
drills.explanation: Use R = E / I. 12 V / 0.5 A = 24 ohms.

drills.title: Power from voltage and current
drills.question: A device uses 13.8 V at 10 A. What is the power?
drills.explanation: Use P = E x I. 13.8 V x 10 A = 138 W.

drills.title: Series resistors
drills.question: What is the total resistance of 10 ohms, 15 ohms and 25 ohms in series?

drills.title: Parallel resistors
drills.question: Two 100 ohm resistors are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance?
drills.explanation: Two equal resistors in parallel equal half one resistor: 100 / 2 = 50 ohms.

drills.title: Wavelength
drills.question: What is the approximate wavelength of a 150 MHz signal?
drills.explanation: Wavelength in metres is approximately 300 / frequency in MHz. 300 / 150 = 2 m.

drills.title: Decibels
drills.question: A 3 dB increase in power is approximately what change?
drills.explanation: For power ratios, +3 dB is approximately x2. -3 dB is approximately half power.

drills.title: Batteries in series
drills.question: Two 12 V, 20 Ah batteries are connected in series. What is the result?
drills.explanation: In series, voltage adds and amp-hour capacity stays the same: 12 + 12 = 24 V, still 20 Ah.

drills.title: Batteries in parallel
drills.question: Two 12 V, 20 Ah batteries are connected in parallel. What is the result?
drills.explanation: In parallel, voltage stays the same and amp-hour capacity adds: 12 V, 20 + 20 = 40 Ah.

drills.title: Reactance trend
drills.question: As frequency increases, what happens to inductive reactance?
drills.explanation: Inductive reactance increases with frequency. Capacitive reactance moves the opposite way.

drills.title: Capacitive reactance trend
drills.question: As frequency increases, what happens to capacitive reactance?
drills.explanation: Capacitive reactance decreases as frequency increases.

drills.title: Metric prefixes
drills.question: What multiplier does the prefix kilo represent?
drills.explanation: Kilo means 1000. Mega means 1,000,000; milli means 0.001; micro means 0.000001.
